goal: Add a live Linux and Windows ComfyUI dashboard monitor for CPU, RAM, swap/pagefile, disk, and individually identified NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel GPUs.

architecture.backend: nodes/system_monitor.py collects cross-platform psutil system metrics; NVIDIA/AMD command adapters provide telemetry, Linux Intel uses DRM sysfs, and Windows uses PowerShell/CIM adapter discovery. It emits snapshots to the ComfyUI websocket and exposes a metadata endpoint.
architecture.frontend: js/dasiwa_system_monitor.js registers a ComfyUI extension, subscribes to monitor snapshots, and renders a compact dashboard with one card per GPU and its GPU ID.

checklist.task: Inspect DaSiWa registration and Crystools monitoring architecture; save implementation checklist.
checklist.evidence: __init__.py, pyproject.toml, Crystools general/{hardware,gpu,monitor}.py and web monitor implementation inspected.

checklist.task: Implement normalized system, swap/pagefile, disk, and multi-vendor GPU telemetry backend.
checklist.evidence: psutil snapshots plus NVIDIA nvidia-smi, AMD rocm-smi JSON, and Intel DRM sysfs adapters implemented.

checklist.task: Implement ComfyUI dashboard UI with per-GPU vendor/name/ID separation.
checklist.evidence: Dashboard creates an individual card for every backend GPU with vendor, GPU ID, load, memory, and temperature.

checklist.task: Load the monitor backend during node-pack initialization.
checklist.evidence: Package initialization imports nodes.system_monitor, registering routes and starting the telemetry worker when ComfyUI is present.

checklist.task: Add adapter/normalization tests and execute focused tests plus Python compilation.
checklist.evidence: pytest tests/test_system_monitor.py -q: 3 passed; py_compile and JSON validation passed; live collector detected NVIDIA:0 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090.

checklist.task: Guarantee Linux and Windows telemetry discovery with a Windows Intel/AMD/NVIDIA fallback.
checklist.evidence: Windows PowerShell/CIM parser implemented and covered by a multi-adapter Intel/AMD fixture. Linux live smoke test detected NVIDIA:0 and returned swap telemetry. Focused test suite: 4 passed.

acceptance_criteria: Dashboard receives CPU, RAM, swap/pagefile, and root-disk usage snapshots.
Every detected GPU is shown independently with vendor, stable GPU ID, name, utilization, memory, and temperature when the vendor reports it.
Linux and Windows use psutil for CPU, RAM, swap/pagefile, and disk usage.
NVIDIA uses nvidia-smi; AMD uses rocm-smi when available; Linux Intel devices use DRM sysfs; Windows uses PowerShell/CIM GPU discovery for NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel adapters.
Unavailable vendor tools or metrics do not break ComfyUI or suppress other telemetry.
validation: Verify emitted snapshot on the running host using the collector.
